# ============================================================
# requirements.txt — cxr-temporal (biovilt + tempavlp)
#
# Generated by surveying every `import` and `from … import` in
# the repo (biovilt/ and tempavlp/).  These versions are known to
# be compatible with each other and with Microsoft's hi-ml-multimodal
# library (which provides the `health_multimodal` package used by
# the BioViL-T image and text encoders).
#
# Recommended setup:
#
#     conda create -n cxrtemporal python=3.10 -y
#     conda activate cxrtemporal
#     pip install -r requirements.txt
#     # Then install hi-ml-multimodal (provides health_multimodal):
#     pip install hi-ml-multimodal
# ============================================================

# ----- Core deep learning -----
# Pin to a torch/torchvision pair that matches; let pip resolve the
# right CUDA/CPU wheel for your machine.
torch>=2.0,<2.4
torchvision>=0.15,<0.19

# ----- HuggingFace stack -----
# Pinned to a range that's compatible with hi-ml-multimodal (which is
# the reason for the upper bound; newer transformers break CXR-BERT).
transformers>=4.30,<4.45
tokenizers>=0.13,<0.20

# ----- Data / IO -----
pandas>=1.5
pillow>=9.0
numpy>=1.23,<2.0
# scipy is pulled in transitively (timm -> scipy). Pin it to a numpy-1.x
# build so it stays consistent with the numpy<2.0 pin above. Without this,
# Colab's pre-installed numpy-2.x-built scipy lingers and raises
# `_blas_supports_fpe` AttributeErrors at import time.
scipy>=1.10,<1.13

# ----- Utilities -----
tqdm>=4.65

# ----- Microsoft's BioViL-T library -----
# Provides the `health_multimodal` package that biovilt/tempcxr/modules/
# image_encoder.py and text_encoder.py import from. Listed separately
# below because it pulls in its own deps; install it AFTER the rest.
# pip install hi-ml-multimodal
